title: Fluorescent Staining of T5-phages
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2osn9","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) for additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Pellet phage in an ultracentrifuge at 28,000 rpm, 90 min, 10ºC in 35 mL polycarbonate tubes, filled to the rim, balanced with phage buffer so that the difference is <0.01 g. 01:30:00 Use the SW-28 rotor, or equivalent, cooled down in the fridge prior to using.
Resuspend the pellet with phage buffer undetermined
If the pellet still seems to have some cellular debris in it, filter the resuspension using a 1 mL syringe and a small 0.2 µm pore size syringe filter.

Re-pellet the viruses in a Beckmann TL-100 ultracentrifuge at 35,000 rpm, 2 hr, 6ºC using the TL-55 rotor cooled down befoer use using 1.4 mL polycarbonate tubes, filled and balanced as the bigger tubes in step 1.
Resuspend the pellet with phage buffer and transfer into 1.5 mL screw cap tubes undetermined
Add YO-PRO stain to each tube containing 100 µL resuspension undetermined Make sure to work in the dark, because the stain is photosensitive.
Cover the tubes with aluminum foil and let sit at 4ºC for 48 hrs
After incubation, increase the volume to 1.4 mL
Centrifuge in the Beckmann TL-100 as in step 5
Suspend the viruses in Milli-Q water undetermined
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
